entries.garrytan/gbrain: This project functions as a self-wiring knowledge graph that uses automated entity extraction and typed linking to organize agent intelligence. It distinguishes itself through a hybrid search system that combines vector retrieval with backlink-boosted ranking and twenty-six specialized skills for data enrichment.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements biomimetic data structures to facilitate long-term learning through dedicated retain, recall, and reflect workflows. It is designed to help agents build evolving mental models and integrates seamlessly with major providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness uses a multi-agent loop to propose, analyze, and curate knowledge for complex tasks. It specializes in self-improvement, distilling validated execution data into optimized runtimes to enhance agent behavior over repeated scenario runs.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A Lossless Context Management plugin that utilizes a hierarchical DAG-based engine to ensure no message is ever lost during compaction. It features three-level escalation logic and immutable message persistence within profile-scoped SQLite databases for structured history retrieval.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This backend provides a private, self-hosted solution for cross-session memory persistence using Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis. It is ideal for users prioritizing data sovereignty, supporting any OpenAI-compatible provider or local inference server.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first context engine that uses a unified SQLite vault to manage memory across different agents without cloud dependencies. It employs a sophisticated hybrid retrieval strategy combining vector search, BM25, and graph traversal to automate decision capture and handoffs.
entries.plur-ai/plur: This shared memory layer utilizes an open YAML 'engram' format to store persistent data locally with zero API costs. It features a unique biological approach to memory management, where information automatically decays or strengthens based on its usage frequency.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: An anticipatory memory system that prefetches relevant context by monitoring agent session logs in real-time. Built as an MCP-native server, it leverages SQLite-vec and GGUF models to provide low-latency RAG capabilities for coding agent clients.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to industry standards like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It enables Hermes to operate across 26 security domains, including malware analysis and cloud security, using the agentskills.io integration standard.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate detectable AI patterns using a three-tier word replacement table. It features a two-pass audit system compatible with Hermes Agent to ensure text remains natural and bypasses AI classifiers.
entries.wondelai/skills: A modular cross-platform library that provides Hermes with specialized frameworks for architecture, UX design, and product strategy. Skills are easily managed via an npx CLI and are compatible with the agentskills.io specification for seamless agent integration.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements type-safe support for the Agent Skills open specification specifically for Pydantic AI. It optimizes Hermes' performance by using progressive disclosure of metadata to significantly reduce token consumption during skill discovery.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill allows Hermes to generate complex draw.io diagrams, such as UML and architecture maps, from natural language descriptions. It supports grid-aligned layouts and animated connectors, providing a visual output layer for technical workflows.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: Designed for blockchain interoperability, this skill enables Hermes to perform CCIP cross-chain sends and interact with multi-chain Data Feeds. It follows the agentskills.io spec to allow secure smart contract monitoring and local testing workflows.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This skill introduces literate programming capabilities, allowing Hermes to weave runnable code and prose into .lit.md files. It includes a tangler to extract source files and automatically syncs edits between documentation and code.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides Hermes with cross-instance shared memory through markdown-based fabric directories. It focuses on 'training your replacement' by extracting decision-quality data to fine-tune and evaluate future model iterations.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: This is a registry and SDK for managing the lifecycle of reusable AI skills with built-in support for Stripe and TON blockchain monetization. It simplifies Hermes' extensibility through automated dependency resolution and local installation of OpenAPI-driven skills.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: The official integration for FLUX image generation, this skill provides Hermes with standardized prompting guidelines and API patterns. It supports precise hex color specification and structured JSON prompting for high-fidelity visual synthesis.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: A meta-skill plugin that monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates both Python scaffolds and documentation, allowing Hermes to programmatically expand its own capabilities.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a local-first harness for long-running tasks that manages structured mission planning and disk-based memory. It enables Hermes to perform fresh 'handoff' launches with preserved context and validated milestone assertions.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill facilitates the autonomous execution of complex, multi-step tasks with built-in checkpoints and recovery procedures. It allows Hermes to track decisions in real-time and perform rollbacks if a task encounters an error.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: A progressive MCP client that uses three-layer lazy loading to minimize token usage for Hermes. It utilizes a socket-based daemon for persistent server connections and supports hot-reloading of configurations without restarts.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: This tool converts repositories and developer discourse into immutable, version-pinned skills for Hermes. It maps AI instructions directly to specific source code lines, preventing documentation rot in agentic workflows.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: A community hub for browsing and installing Hermes skills with automated security scanning for prompt injections. It features a micropayment protocol for premium skills and an agent-to-agent feedback system based on proof-of-use reviews.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project brings the agentskills.io specification to the .NET ecosystem, allowing Hermes to interface with C# MCP tools. It implements progressive disclosure to save tokens while supporting automatic skill discovery from vendor directories.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides a collection of daily-updated Nix packages for AI coding agents, ensuring reproducible and declarative environments for Hermes. It is ideal for users who need consistent versioning across both open-source tools and proprietary binary distributions.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw focuses on high-security deployments by implementing hardware-level enforcement of network, filesystem, and process boundaries. It includes three security policy presets and supports over 40 built-in tools, including MCP integration and persistent memory.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This repository offers a dedicated NixOS module for managing Hermes configurations, documents, and systemd services through a declarative framework. It prioritizes security with sops-nix integration for secrets handling and supports a hybrid skill model for both Nix-managed and interactive installs.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this no-install desktop agent provides a GUI and over 100 tools without requiring admin rights or Docker. It features integrated GPU monitoring, local model management, and a workflow engine for scheduled or conditional automation.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project provides a streamlined Docker sandbox that includes the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It supports persistent state through mounted home directories and allows users to build specific Hermes versions using build arguments.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeted at production environments, this tool configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service for Ubuntu and Debian servers. It is optimized for headless operation and enables the autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs via Nous Portal integration.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This Docker Compose stack integrates Hermes with a ttyd web terminal for browser-based access and management via the Portainer UI. It simplifies deployment by automating image builds through GitHub Actions and the GitHub Container Registry.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen with zero external dependencies. It is essential for Hermes users who require a private environment to monitor task spend and implement human-in-the-loop sign-offs via the Aegis review system.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw provides a self-hosted runtime for building autonomous agent swarms that support 23 different model providers, including Hermes and local LLMs. It distinguishes itself through persistent agent memory and a unique 'conversation-to-skill' learning mechanism that expands agent capabilities over time.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This specialized framework focuses on meta-optimization by using an automated outer-loop search to improve the performance of Hermes Agent on benchmarks. It orchestrates structured candidate mutations to find the most efficient harness code for TBLite and TB2 evaluation tasks.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent, compete for GitHub stars in real-time. The system orchestrates nine specialized agents and visualizes their tasks, code generation, and scoreboard metrics through a live dashboard.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This framework coordinates 17 specialized agents across distinct development phases ranging from research and planning to infrastructure. It ensures high-quality outputs by implementing mandatory quality gates and code reviews at every stage of the multi-agent lifecycle.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native software development lifecycle by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It enforces architectural integrity through an eight-phase process where each transition is protected by automated graph-diff phase gates.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This project enables ACP-style delegation by routing tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing parent context pollution. It prioritizes safety and efficiency through isolated workflows, configurable timeouts, and strict output character limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based TUI tracks token consumption and real-time costs across over 20 AI coding agents, including Hermes and Claude Code. It leverages LiteLLM pricing data to provide developers with detailed 3D visualizations and usage analytics for their local and cloud-based workflows.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows developers to personalize the Hermes terminal experience through custom YAML-based themes and ASCII banners. It features a flexible inheritance system that lets users override specific UI elements like spinners and color schemes while maintaining the core interface functionality.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and its 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel, eliminating the need for external processes or OAuth. It is particularly valuable for developers requiring native MLOps capabilities, such as vLLM inference management and reinforcement learning training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser designed for autonomous control, Vessel exposes structured page schemas and durable session states via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It bridges the gap between agents and the web by providing a supervisory sidebar for human auditing and manual intervention during complex workflows.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: Ankh.md introduces a scoped framework for Hermes, allowing developers to define project-specific memories, tools, and instructions within local folders. It enables seamless switching between global settings and specialized agent configurations tailored to the unique requirements of a single workspace.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: This static analysis tool identifies structural flaws and ambiguities in agent configurations and system prompts without incurring LLM API costs. By detecting failure modes like tool loops, it serves as an automated quality gate for integrating Hermes agents into CI/CD pipelines.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility streamlines the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes by mapping complex model configurations and personas into the Hermes YAML format. It automates the transfer of workspace files, credentials, and memories to ensure a consistent agent experience across platforms.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This deployment stack uses Docker to orchestrate a complete Hermes environment featuring LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for local memory. It is optimized for zero-cost operations, providing 29 pre-configured plugins and automated routing for free model inference.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This zero-framework interface provides a mobile-friendly, three-panel layout for managing chats, files, and sessions. It maintains full parity with the Hermes CLI while using only Python and vanilla JavaScript for a lightweight, dependency-free experience.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A security-hardened workspace that features real-time SSE streaming and integrated rendering for tool calls. It specializes in large-scale agent management, offering tools to browse memory and oversee a library of over 2,000 skills.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: This self-hosted dashboard acts as a multi-agent gateway for configuring and monitoring various agent profiles. It stands out for its detailed token analytics, which track costs across different models and platforms in real-time.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS application that establishes direct SSH connections to agents without requiring a middle-layer API or remote daemon. It provides a multi-tabbed terminal environment for managing remote cron jobs, skills, and SQLite session data.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: Built as a React-based Telegram Mini App, this mobile-first dashboard enables background agent spawning and live output monitoring. It features a terminal-style chat interface alongside system health gauges for tracking CPU and memory usage on the go.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This desktop companion simplifies the onboarding process through guided installation and dependency resolution for Hermes Agent. It supports a wide range of LLM providers and includes integrated messaging gateways for platforms like Discord and Telegram.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A monitoring-focused dashboard that provides real-time activity tracking and cost distribution analysis. It features deep conversation inspection powered by FTS5 search and a read-only view for managing scheduled cron jobs.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-first workspace that integrates an execution timeline for tools and a marketplace for skills and MCP servers. It allows for complete isolation between sessions by managing separate profiles, memory stores, and API keys.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: This glassmorphic web interface uses a single-file React architecture that requires no build step for deployment. It includes a dedicated artifact panel for live rendering of HTML, SVG, and code generated during agent sessions.
